Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read]: Search Linear for all engineering team tickets in backlog or deprioritized state using linear_list_issues (team: "Engineering" or team filter for engineering team, state: "backlog" or similar). Expected findings: ENG-187 (Invoice email template safety checks, prevent attachment mismatches, state: backlog, priority 2, assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom, due April 18), ENG-210 ([v2] ML-based CategoryMatcher, shadow mode experiment, state: backlog, priority 4, assignee: Dmitri Volkov, deprioritized post-pilot), ENG-213 (Dashboard: Bulk export & policy version history, state: backlog, priority 3, assignee: Anh Nguyen, de-prioritized for pilot), and ENG-201 through ENG-204 (epic containers, state: backlog, unassigned).
OE 2 [Read]: Get details and read comments on ENG-187 using linear_get_issue and linear_list_comments (issueId for ENG-187). Expected findings: Description explains that manual invoice emails do not validate PDF attachment matches QuickBooks invoice/customer/recipient, safeguard opened after the Terraform pricing leak. Comments reveal: (1) Lena deferred on April 6, saying she cannot take it while on DOT compliance tied to Raj Patel paperwork, leaving it unassigned for Samira to re-slot; (2) Samira acknowledged keeping it in backlog on April 6; (3) Samira's April 15 comment connecting the Sunbelt/Palmetto exposure to this missing control, admits she approved deprioritizing for DOT; (4) Marcus's April 15 comment confirming QB-vs-emailed-PDF mismatch and requesting engineering input on a safeguard; (5) Marcus's April 22 comment escalating this as the second pricing-exposure event tied to an unbuilt safeguard.
OE 3 [Read]: Search Slack for the swapped invoices incident using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "PDF" or similar). Expected findings: Hana Kim's April 11 message in #finance about sending Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices back-to-back; David Chen's April 16 message asking Emeka about Raj Greenfield's email regarding nonprofit pricing; Marcus Thorne's analysis in #finance confirming QuickBooks records are correct but the PDF attachments were crossed, Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate invoice ($2,400 with Palmetto header) and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial-rate invoice ($4,800); Emeka's April 23 reconstruction in #customer-engagement confirming INV-2026-0398 ($4,800 Sunbelt) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400 Palmetto) are correct in the database but the email attachments were swapped on April 10.
OE 4 [Read]: Search emails for the invoice swap incident using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "INV-2026-0398" or "invoice" or similar). Expected findings: Hana's original sent emails (INV-2026-0398 to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com, INV-2026-0399 to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org); Raj Greenfield's reply complaining the PDF shows $2,400 with Palmetto Community Foundation header instead of his $4,800 Sunbelt invoice; Carmen Delgado-Reyes' reply flagging the $4,800 amount as an audit risk for her 501(c)(3) nonprofit; Marcus Thorne's internal urgent email to David, Hana, and Emeka confirming QB is fine but PDFs crossed; Samira Tariq's email to Marcus and David (CC Lena) providing engineering context about the dormant invoice attachment safeguard ticket that Lena pushed back on April 6.
OE 5 [Read]: Get details on other backlog engineering tickets using linear_get_issue for ENG-210 and ENG-213 (and optionally ENG-201 through ENG-204). Expected findings: ENG-210 is the ML-based CategoryMatcher v2 shadow mode experiment, deprioritized with note "Deprioritized for now", this does not map to a specific real-world incident. ENG-213 is Dashboard bulk export and policy version history, de-prioritized for pilot, this also does not map to a specific incident. ENG-201 through ENG-204 are completed epic containers (Core Categorization Engine, Client Policy Configuration, Admin Dashboard, Integration Testing) sitting in backlog as organizational shells, not deprioritized work items with active gaps.
OE 6 [Read]: Search Airtable for Mosaic Robotics stipend records using airtable_list_bases to find base appMoveOpsOps001, then airtable_list_tables to find tblStipends00001 (Stipend Transactions), then airtable_search_records (field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics") or airtable_list_records filtered for Mosaic. Expected findings: Three records still showing "Approved" that were falsely auto-approved by the broken Mosaic config, recStip000000021 (Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200, policy cap is $800, string comparison bug), recStip000000022 (Andre Washington, Coworking Space Membership, $350, coworking explicitly excluded from Mosaic policy, excluded_categories config bug), recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Day Passes, $135, same exclusion bug). Additional flagged records: rec_stipend_andre_desk (Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200, "Approved", notes: "INCORRECTLY APPROVED, Previously flagged in pilot audit ENG-216") and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork (Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Passes, $135, "Approved", notes: "INCORRECTLY APPROVED, Previously flagged in pilot audit ENG-216").
OE 7 [Read]: Search Airtable for Vectral Systems stipend records using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems"). Expected findings: Three records still showing "Rejected" that were falsely rejected by the broken Vectral config, recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, Standing Desk, $550, stale $400 default limit, Vectral policy allows $600, "Pending manual correction"), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, Ergonomic Keyboard Tray, $82, double bug: miscategorized as office_supplies and used stale $50 limit, "Pending manual correction"), recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, Mesh WiFi System, $130, stale $100 default limit, Vectral policy allows $150, "Pending manual correction").
OE 8 [Read]: Search Slack for ExpenseBot financial exposure discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "ExpenseBot" or "false approval" or "pilot audit" or "$885" or similar). Expected findings: Dmitri's audit results in #engineering, 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct (83%), 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 total financial exposure; Emeka's report of Mosaic false approvals mentioning $1,550 exposure from malformed JSON config; Marcus's financial concern in #finance about the out-of-policy approvals.
OE 9 [Read]: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices to verify financial data tied to the swap incident. Expected findings: INV-2026-0398 ($4,800, customer: Sunbelt Venture Partners, date: April 10, due: May 10) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400, customer: Palmetto Community Foundation, date: April 10, due: May 10), confirming the QuickBooks records are correct and the error was solely in the PDF attachments sent via email on April 10.
OE 10 [Read]: Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq") or contacts_get_contacts. Expected finding: samira.tariq@moveops.com (contact_id: moveops_samira_tariq).
OE 11 [Read]: Look up Slack channel list using channels_list to find the #engineering channel ID. Expected finding: channel C003 (name: engineering).
OE 12 [Write]: Update ENG-187 priority to P1 using linear_update_issue (id: the issue ID for ENG-187, priority: 1). This bumps the invoice attachment safeguard ticket from its current backlog/priority-2 state to urgent, reflecting the direct connection to the Sunbelt/Palmetto swap, the incident it was designed to prevent.
OE 13 [Write]: Add a comment to ENG-187 documenting the real-world incident using linear_create_comment (issueId: the issue ID for ENG-187, body: documenting the connection between the missing attachment validation safeguard and the April 10 Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap). Comment should include: Hana Kim's April 10 batch send crossed PDF attachments between INV-2026-0398 (Sunbelt, $4,800) and INV-2026-0399 (Palmetto, $2,400); Sunbelt's Raj Greenfield saw the nonprofit-rate document exposing MoveOps' differential pricing; Palmetto's Carmen Delgado-Reyes flagged the inflated amount as audit risk for her 501(c)(3); Marcus Thorne identified this as the second pricing-exposure event after the Terraform reply-all; the financial exposure includes reputational pricing damage to both client relationships.
OE 14 [Write]: Update the falsely approved Mosaic Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: update Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" or "Under Review" for recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023 and/or rec_stipend_andre_desk, rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork). These are the Mosaic false approvals, Andre Washington's standing desk ($1,200 exceeding $800 cap) and coworking ($350 in excluded category), and Kenji Nakamura's coworking passes ($135 in excluded category).
OE 15 [Write]: Update the falsely rejected Vectral Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: update Approval Status from "Rejected" to "Approved" and set appropriate Approved Amounts for recStip000000018 (Maria Santos, $550), recStip000000019 (James Okafor, $82), recStip000000020 (Lisa Park, $130)). These were auto-rejected by ExpenseBot using stale default limits when Vectral's actual policy allows the submitted amounts.
OE 16 [Write]: Send email to Samira Tariq using send_email (sender: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, recipients: [samira.tariq@moveops.com], subject: engineering backlog audit, preventable incidents and financial exposure (or similar)). Email content should cover: (1) ENG-187 (attachment safeguard) directly maps to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap, the safeguard was deprioritized April 6 and the swap happened April 10, exposing differential pricing to Sunbelt and creating nonprofit audit risk for Palmetto; (2) Airtable stipend records still in incorrect state, 3 Mosaic false approvals totaling $1,685 still showing "Approved" and 3 Vectral false rejections totaling $762 still showing "Rejected" with "Pending manual correction" notes; (3) other backlog tickets (ENG-210, ENG-213) do not map to specific incidents but remain unresolved; (4) total financial exposure: pricing reputational damage from the swap, $885 confirmed pilot exposure, $1,685 in incorrectly approved Mosaic expenses; (5) recommendation to immediately prioritize ENG-187 and address the stipend record corrections.
OE 17 [Write]: Post a summary in #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003, payload: summary of backlog audit findings). Summary should include: ENG-187 gap led directly to the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap, Airtable stipend records from the pilot audit remain in incorrect states, ticket has been bumped to P1, and a call for the team to address the engineering debt that allowed these preventable problems to ship.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
